behaviors.description: The conversation history sent to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (history builder -> LLM client payload), not pure single-function logic only. Best test is wiring-level with a stub LLM transport that captures request payload. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment/content quality. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only requires payload shape/length ('passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages'), not real model output. Adversarial critique: if trimming is fully encapsulated in one deterministic helper and no handoff bugs are plausible, Unit could be enough.
behaviors.test_description: Run chatbot request path with >10 prior messages and assert outgoing LLM request contains exactly last 10 in correct order/roles; assert <=10 histories are unchanged.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer question, not generic/off-topic output.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality (relevance/on-topicness). A stubbed model would always pass and hide real failures (deterministic-assertion trap). REAL-INFRA may be HTTP, but the critical boundary is model judgment, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes a single generation quality check, not a required multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use a labeled set of customer questions and run real model inference; score each reply for topical relevance (human rubric or deterministic evaluator threshold) and fail if relevance falls below acceptance target.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects when a draft is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly includes correct tone identification, which depends on model judgment. Mocking tone-check output can validate trigger wiring but cannot catch misclassification failures. MULTI-STEP exists (detection then regeneration), but minimum real-failure boundary is one model decision governing handoff, so Agentic. Rejected Workflow: text does not require validating quality of both generations end-to-end, only that too-formal is identified and regeneration is triggered. Adversarial critique: if production bugs mostly occur in multi-step orchestration/state carryover between first and second calls, Workflow might be necessary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed casual-tier prompts likely to induce overly formal first drafts; run real tone-check path and assert system flags formality and issues regeneration call, with audit marker showing first draft rejected.

behaviors.description: User message, generated response, and any regenerations are persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is persistence wiring across chatbot service + repository + DB schema/transaction logic. A real local test DB is needed to catch mapping/order/timestamp propagation issues; no external OS/network/subprocess behavior is inherently required beyond local DB integration. LLM output quality is irrelevant. Rejected System: behavior text is about persistence correctness, not real networked DB outages/timeouts. Adversarial critique: if timestamps depend on DB server clock/timezone or trigger behavior only present in deployed DB engine, System could be required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute request flows with and without regeneration against a test DB; assert stored rows include all exchanges in correct sequence and timestamps are present, correctly ordered, and mapped to each attempt.

behaviors.description: If tone-check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first so the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff/output selection between generation stages and delivery path. This can be caught by integration wiring with controlled model outputs and forced tone-trigger state. Rejected Agentic/Workflow by behavior text: condition is explicit ('When the tone check triggers regeneration'), so test can inject trigger and verify replacement semantics without relying on real model judgment quality. Rejected System: no required real subprocess/OS/network behavior is specified. Adversarial critique: if customer delivery is streamed over real transport and first draft can leak before replacement, System (or Workflow with real model timing) may be needed.
behaviors.test_description: Force first draft + tone-trigger + second draft in end-to-end service wiring (stub model responses allowed) and assert delivered customer payload equals second draft only; assert first draft exists only in internal logs/audit and is never emitted on customer channel.
